English: My grandfather, a man by the name of White Mexican Clansman, and another man called The Son of Former Sweathouse, were the only men. They and several womenfolk worked hard on me.
Navajo: Shichei ńt’éé’ Naakai Dine’é Łigaii wolyéé ńt’éé’ dóó ła’ dó’ Diné Táchéhéni’ Biye’ wolyéé ńt’éé’ ’éí shį́į́ t’éiyá diné nilį́įgo dóó sáanii dó’ díkwíí shį́į́ kwii shaa naanish bííghą́ą́’
[Shichei (nv), my grandfather (en), shi- → 1.SG.POSS: chei → maternal.grandfather | ńt’éé’ (nv), past (en), ńt’éé’ → PST | Naakai (nv), Mexican (en), Naakai → Mexican | Dine’é (nv), people (en), dine’é → people | Łigaii (nv), white (en), łigaii → white | wolyéé (nv), is called (en), wo- → DIR: l- → L: yéé → call.NEU.IMPF | ńt’éé’ (nv), past (en), ńt’éé’ → PST | dóó (nv), and (en), dóó → and | ła’ (nv), one (en), ła’ → one | dó’ (nv), also (en), dó’ → also | Diné (nv), people (en), diné → person | Táchéhéni’ (nv), former sweathouse (en), Táchéhéni’ → Former.Sweathouse | Biye’ (nv), his son (en), bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ye’ → son | wolyéé (nv), he is called (en), wo- → DIR: l- → L: yéé → call.NEU.IMPF | ńt’éé’ (nv), past (en), ńt’éé’ → PST | ’éí (nv), that (en), ’éí → that | shį́į́ (nv), probably (en), shį́į́ → probably | t’éiyá (nv), only (en), t’éiyá → only | diné (nv), people (en), diné → person | nilį́įgo (nv), being (en), ni- → TH: lį́į → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | dóó (nv), and (en), dóó → and | sáanii (nv), women (en), sáanii → women | dó’ (nv), also (en), dó’ → also | díkwíí (nv), several (en), díkwíí → several | shį́į́ (nv), probably (en), shį́į́ → probably | kwii (nv), here about (en), kwii → here.about | shaa (nv), on me (en), sh- → 1.SG.POSS: aa → to | naanish (nv), they work (en), naa- → CONT: nish → work.CONT.IMPF | bííghą́ą́’ (nv), it killed them (en), b- → 3.BI: íí- → 3.YI.PERF: ghą́ą́’ → kill.DU/PL.PERF]

English: So then my grandfather, the man I spoke of as Big Mexican Clansman, volunteered to join him

English: That is how it came about that the one who would be called The Interpreter (Chee Dodge) and the one who would be named Big Lump Setting Up came to move there.

[Shichei (nv), my grandfather (en), shi- → 1.SG.POSS: chei → maternal.grandfather | yę́ę (nv), former (en), =yę́ę → =PST.REF | ’ákót’éego (nv), while it being so (en), ’ákó- → so: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | t’áá (nv), just (en), t’áá → PCL | nashineeyood (nv), he drove me (en), na- → CONT: shi- → 1.SG: n- → TH: ee- → 3.NI.PERF: yood → drive.CONT.PERF | dóó (nv), and (en), dóó → and | t’áá (nv), just (en), t’áá → PCL | shich’į’ (nv), toward me (en), shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ch’į’ → toward | yááłti’ (nv), he spoke (en), yá- → TH: áá- → 3.YI.PERF: ł- → Ł: ti’ → speak.PERF]

English: It was there that they carried on the ceremony.
Navajo: ’Áájį’ shį́į́ łee’íyátééh ha’nínígíí baa njizh’áázh

English: It was there that my grandfather spoke and promised us.

English: "Say, let's go to school," I said to my companion.

English: "Come on, you boys. Remember what you said," said Black Horse.

English: It was reported that the children from Chinle who were being taken to school would get there in two days, and we heard the grownups talking to one another about it.

English: A man of the Salty Water Clan whose name was Slender Lava had his family there, and his wife cooked all night for the men who were making the trouble.

English: Among those present were the men known as Black Horse, Limper, Tall Red House Clansman, Old Bead Chant Singer - who was of the Red House Clan - and his cousin, a Bitter Water Clansman called Bead Chant's Nephew, and another man with whom I was merely acquainted, and who was a relative of mine by marriage. His name was Yellow Fermentive Chewer. These are the only ones I can remember of those who came from there (on the other side of the mountain).

English: His grandparents are like teachers to him. He asks them how The People used to live, and they tell him.
Navajo: Hachaii da nayízhdíłkidgo hanálí da nayízhdíłkidgo hamá sání da ńléidą́ą́ yik’ehgo dayíkaii ’índa badahane’ da danilíinii ’éí bázhdiits’a’go ’ákót’éego bá ’ajółta’ nahalin

Navajo: Yoo’ó ’Adika’í yę́ę lá Tséhootsooídę́ę́’ Hastiin Deez Hastiin ’Anilí ni’ Biye’ dó’ wolyéé ńt’éé’

English: A man by the name of Black Horse was the leader of that faction.

English: There was a man by the name of Limper, who may still be hunched with old age (still alive). There was one called Slow One, and there were Canyon De Chelly Man, Tall Bitter Water Clansman, ugly Knife, Sucker, Weela, and Gray Haired Man. English: When they spread out into the brush we would just let them go and follow them.

English: I got over to where the dog was barking, and there I saw a bear standing.
Navajo: Łééchąą’í ndahał’inę́ęjį’ yishwoł ńt’éé kǫ́ǫ́ shash sizį́įgo yiiłtsą́
[Łééchąą’í (nv), dog (en), łééchąą’í → dog | ndahał’inę́ęjį’ (nv), to where it was barking (en), n- → CONT: da- → DISTR: ha- → AR: ł- → Ł: ’in → bark.CONT.IMPF: =ę́ę → =PST.REF: =jį’ → =up.to | yishwoł (nv), I was running (en), yish- → 1.SG.PROG: woł → run.PROG | ńt’éé (nv), PST (en), ńt’éé → PST | kǫ́ǫ́ (nv), through here (en), kǫ́ǫ́ → through.here | shash (nv), bear (en), shash → bear | sizį́įgo (nv), that he was standing (en), si- → 3.SI.PERF: zį́į → stand.SG.NEU.PERF: =go → =SUB | yiiłtsą́ (nv), I glimpsed (en), y- → 3.YI: ii- → 1.SG.YI.ZERO.PERF: ł- → Ł: tsą́ → glimpse.PERF]

English: I found that out.

English: Sometimes the rams would get far apart and my cousin would step in between them and then jump aside as their heads came together.

English: Sometimes the rams would get far apart and my cousin would step in between them and then jump aside as their heads came together.
Navajo: Łahda ńléí doo deeghánídę́ę́’ deenásts’aa’ ’ałts’ą́ą́’ ninát’ashgo ’aadę́ę́’ ’ałch’į’ náádiilwo’go bita’ góne’ ’aníjílwo’go t’áá ’ahánídę́ę́’ ’ahinoolchéełgo ’índa níláahjį’ ch’ééjiilyeedgo kót’éego da baa neit’aash łeh ńt’éé’
[Łahda (nv), sometimes (en), łahda → sometimes | ńléí (nv), over there (en), ńléí → over.there | doo deeghánídę́ę́’ (nv), far apart (en), doo deeghání → far: =dę́ę́’ → =from | deenásts’aa’ (nv), ram (en), deenásts’aa’ → ram | ’ałts’ą́ą́’ (nv), separated from each other (en), ’ał- → REC.POSS: ts’ą́ą́’ → separated | ninát’ashgo (nv), while they would both go (en), ni- → stop: ná- → ITER: t- → D: ’ash → go.DU.ITER: =go → =SUB | ’aadę́ę́’ (nv), from there (en), ’aa → there: =dę́ę́’ → =from | ’ałch’į’ (nv), toward one another (en), ’ał- → REC.POSS: ch’į’ → toward | náádiilwo’go (nv), while they would start running (en), náá- → SITR: d- → TH: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.IMPF: l- → L: wo’ → run.ITER: =go → =SUB | bita’ (nv), between them (en), bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ta’ → between | góne’ (nv), inside (en), góne’ → inside | ’aníjílwo’go (nv), while one would run (en), ’a- → TH: ní- → ITER: jí- → ANIM: l- → L: wo’ → run.ITER: =go → =SUB | t’áá ’ahánídę́ę́’ (nv), from near (en), t’áá ’áhání → near: =dę́ę́’ → =from | ’ahinoolchéełgo (nv), when they would be running (en), ’ahi- → REC: n- → TH: oo- → 3.PROG: l- → L: chéeł → run.PROG: =go → =SUB | ’índa (nv), then (en), ’índa → then | níláahjį’ (nv), over there (en), níláah → over.there: =jį’ → =up.to | ch’ééjiilyeedgo (nv), when he would run back up (en), ch’- → out: éé- → REV: j- → ANIM: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.IMPF: l- → L: yeed → run.IMPF: =go → =SUB | kót’éego (nv), it is thus (en), kót’ée → it.is.thus: =go → =SUB | da (nv), like (en), da → indefinite | baa (nv), about it (en), bi- → 3.BI.POSS: aa → to | neit’aash (nv), we are going around (en), ne- → CONT: i- → 1.DU/PL.ZERO.IMPF: t- → D: ’aash → go.DU.IMPF | łeh (nv), usually (en), łeh → usually | ńt’éé’ (nv), past (en), ńt’éé’ → PST]

[’Áko (nv), so (en), ’áko → so | diné (nv), people (en), diné → person | lą’í (nv), many (en), lą’ → be.many.NEU.IMPF: =í → =NMLZ | dził (nv), mountain (en), dził → mountain | yighą́ą́’ (nv), its back (en), yi- → 3.YI.POSS: ghą́ą́’ → back | hadahineesdzood (nv), they drove up one after another (en), ha- → TH: da- → DISTR: hi- → SER: n- → TH: ees- → 3.SI.PERF: d- → D: yood → drive.PERF]

English: Then we followed the ridge up over Black Mountain.
Navajo: ’Aadóó ’eii Dziłíjiin dah sitą́ t’áá bikáá’góó náániikai

English: They were to go by way of Lukachukai, Tsaile, and Crystal, and on over to Fort Defiance.

English: In the evening, on our return trip, one of the motors caught fire. It happened between Flagstaff and Phoenix.

English: In the evening after we had unloaded another load of wood we started back home in the wagon.

English: These big planes have four motors.
